ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.description: Organic ginger root sourced from family farms in Kerala, India. Known for its digestive support properties and traditional use for nausea relief.
ingredients.traditionalUse: Used for thousands of years in Ayurvedic medicine for digestive complaints, nausea, and inflammation.
ingredients.cultivation: Grown in the rich, volcanic soil of Kerala's Western Ghats region using traditional organic farming methods passed down through generations.
ingredients.harvesting: Hand-harvested at peak maturity to ensure maximum gingerol content.
ingredients.testing: Each batch tested for gingerol content, heavy metals, pesticides, and microbial contamination.

ingredients.name: Ashwagandha
ingredients.description: Premium ashwagandha root from the arid regions of Rajasthan, where the harsh climate produces plants with higher withanolide concentrations.
ingredients.traditionalUse: One of the most important herbs in Ayurveda, used for over 3,000 years as a rasayana (rejuvenative) for vitality and longevity.
ingredients.cultivation: Grown in Rajasthan's sandy, well-drained soils using sustainable dry-farming techniques that stress the plants naturally, increasing potency.
ingredients.harvesting: Roots harvested after 150-180 days when withanolide content peaks.
ingredients.testing: Standardized to 5% withanolides. Tested for heavy metals and authenticity.

ingredients.name: Turmeric
ingredients.description: High-curcumin turmeric from the Erode district of Tamil Nadu, known as the 'Turmeric City' of India.
ingredients.traditionalUse: Central to Ayurvedic medicine for thousands of years, used for inflammation, digestion, and as a sacred spice in ceremonies.
ingredients.cultivation: Grown in Tamil Nadu's red soil using traditional crop rotation with rice paddies, enriching the soil naturally.
ingredients.harvesting: Rhizomes harvested 7-9 months after planting when curcumin content is highest.
ingredients.testing: Standardized to 95% curcuminoids. Enhanced with black pepper extract for bioavailability.

ingredients.name: Moringa
ingredients.description: Nutrient-dense moringa leaves from Andhra Pradesh, where the warm climate produces leaves with exceptional nutritional profiles.
ingredients.traditionalUse: Called the 'Miracle Tree' in Ayurveda, used for nutrition, energy, and supporting lactation for centuries.
benefits: Rich in vitamins A, C, E
ingredients.cultivation: Grown on small family farms using organic practices. Trees are pruned regularly to encourage tender, nutrient-rich leaf growth.
ingredients.harvesting: Young leaves harvested by hand in the early morning when nutrient content is highest.
ingredients.testing: Tested for nutrient content, heavy metals, and microbial contamination.

ingredients.name: Shatavari
ingredients.description: Wild-crafted shatavari from the foothills of the Himalayas, where pristine conditions produce roots with exceptional saponin content.
ingredients.traditionalUse: Known as the 'Queen of Herbs' in Ayurveda, traditionally used to support women's reproductive health across all life stages.
ingredients.cultivation: Sustainably wild-crafted from the Himalayan foothills, ensuring minimal environmental impact and maximum potency.
ingredients.harvesting: Roots harvested from mature plants (3+ years) during the dry season.
ingredients.testing: Standardized to 40% saponins. Tested for identity, potency, and purity.

ingredients.name: Holy Basil (Tulsi)
ingredients.description: Sacred tulsi from the plains of Uttar Pradesh, grown in temple gardens and family farms using generations-old organic methods.
ingredients.traditionalUse: Revered as 'The Incomparable One' in Ayurveda, used for stress, respiratory health, and spiritual practices.
ingredients.cultivation: Grown in sacred groves and organic farms using traditional polyculture methods that honor the plant's spiritual significance.
ingredients.harvesting: Leaves and flowering tops harvested by hand, dried in shade to preserve volatile compounds.
ingredients.testing: Standardized to 2.5% ursolic acid. Tested for identity and purity.

ingredients.name: Black Pepper
ingredients.description: Premium Malabar black pepper from Kerala, the original home of the 'King of Spices'.
ingredients.traditionalUse: Used in Ayurveda to enhance bioavailability of other herbs and support digestion.
ingredients.cultivation: Grown on traditional pepper vines in Kerala's spice gardens, using shade trees and organic mulching.
ingredients.harvesting: Berries harvested when they begin to turn red, then sun-dried to develop characteristic flavor.
ingredients.testing: Standardized to 95% piperine for maximum bioavailability enhancement.

regions.name: Kerala
regions.description: Known as the 'Spice Garden of India', Kerala's Western Ghats provide ideal conditions for ginger and pepper cultivation.
regions.climate: Tropical monsoon climate with high rainfall

regions.name: Rajasthan
regions.description: The arid climate of Rajasthan naturally stresses plants, producing ashwagandha with higher concentrations of active compounds.

regions.name: Tamil Nadu
regions.description: The Erode district is known as the 'Turmeric City', producing some of India's finest turmeric.
regions.climate: Tropical with distinct wet and dry seasons

regions.name: Andhra Pradesh
regions.description: Warm climate and fertile soil create ideal conditions for moringa cultivation.

regions.name: Himalayan Foothills
regions.description: Pristine mountain environment produces wild-crafted herbs of exceptional quality.

regions.name: Uttar Pradesh
regions.description: Traditional growing region for holy basil, with deep cultural and spiritual significance.
